parsed_review.summary: CP00-242 (RP07.P02.M05 Permission And Audit Binding middle rows; risk_class A; 10 units S11-S20) is a clean, internally consistent descriptor-only closeout pack. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted: manifest.json holds production_ready:false / status implemented_pending_review, and the production_ready_flag (search_core_service_binding_mid_descriptor_verified) denotes descriptor verification, not runtime or enterprise-trust readiness. Implementation (registry binding, service freeze/case-set/descriptor builders, validators coverage+descriptor assertions), contract (service_binding_mid_descriptor block, lines ~7983-8331), tests (model.test.js CP242 trio), and the RP07 validation script are mutually aligned on 10 units, RP07.P02.M05.S11-S20, all no-runtime/no-real-data flags false, customer-safe errors only, and the five no-leak guards (validation_error_detail/blocked_claim_detail/lock_token excluded, unauthorized_data omitted, raw_payload excluded). Local/Hermes validation evidence passes CP178+ hardening: auth_preflight exit 0 with 1456-byte valid JSON, readiness ready:true, validation-report confirms Read/Grep/Glob only with no forbidden tools and read_tools_used inspection basis; command-evidence shows all validation commands exit 0 with no_real_data:true and writes_product_state:false. Claude is correctly bound as a read-only, non-final-approval reviewer (claude_is_final_approval:false; promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false; human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true; runtime opens at CP00-243), and Hermes control-plane patterns appear only in the deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false throughout), not product runtime. Unrelated dirty UI/docs files are correctly isolated out of pack scope per changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
